items.title: Master Artifact selection and five natural components
items.rationale: Frontrunner is the Heirloom Timepiece (escapement / dial / hands / gear-train / pendulum), but the artifact is locked only after the regional workshop and cultural consultation. The agent must not invent the final choice; this stays PENDING until the team records it in data/artifacts/packets/artifact_lock.json.

items.title: Carrier compatibility policy (>=3 compatible candidates per fragment)
items.rationale: How openable templates map to fragments so every fragment has at least three compatible carrier candidates (CLAUDE.md §4-C, §4-M). Tuning is a team call after the full openable catalog is scoped in Phase 13-14.

items.title: Decoy density (ordinary flickering openables per loop)
items.rationale: Number of ordinary openable decoys placed alongside the promoted carrier each loop, so the carrier is found by the heartbeat band rather than by being the only openable (§4-I). Awaiting playtest tuning.

items.title: Pile size cap (max openables per pile)
items.rationale: Maximum openables a single pile/container may hold during triage and discovery. Awaiting triage/spawn tuning.

items.title: Full-screen UI clock policy
items.rationale: The day clock pauses while a full-screen/blocking UI is open, using the existing ref-counted pause-ownership API on DayClock (CLAUDE.md §4-J). This was resolved in-fiction and in code; recorded here as the versioned design decision.

items.title: Locked-container gating for fragments
items.rationale: Whether and how locked crates / the Safe gate fragment access, while keeping every placement winnable that run (§4-H). A known Safe code may make the Safe eligible as an outer container, but the fragment still sits in a promoted ordinary carrier. Final policy is a team call.

items.title: Buyer fifth-fragment release behavior
items.rationale: The Buyer's qualifying Day 5 encounter releases the fifth fragment into a guaranteed special delivery; no character hands a fragment over directly (CLAUDE.md §4-B). Resolved in-fiction; recorded here as versioned design data.
